action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
My tummy gives a rumbling sound,
Like a drum inside a hollow ground.
Mom says, "Eat an apple, have a pear!"
But I want a cookie, right there, right there!
I grab the chocolate, so big and sweet,
And pretend I'm healthy, looking neat.
But the sugar rush is making me zoom,
Like a rocket ship in a sugar room!

SECTION 2
My teeth start aching, oh no, oh no!
It feels like a tiny, sharp dragon blowing snow.
The candy melts and sticks to the side,
Making a sticky, sugary hide.
The little sugar bugs are marching round,
Chewing up the enamel like a sound!
One tooth is missing, two more might go,
If I keep eating sweets, where will I grow?

SECTION 3
Now my feet are heavy, they won't lift up high,
My knees are shaking and my eyes are all sleepy.
That chocolate bar made me run slow,
Like a turtle stuck in a mudhole flow.
I tripped on the rug and fell on the floor,
And my stomach made a loud "Burp, Burp, Whoosh!"
Now I'm full and stuffed, I can't move a foot,
Just floating in a sugar bubble suit.

SECTION 4
So tomorrow morning, when you wake up bright,
You might get a tummy ache in the middle of the night.
Or maybe you'll get a rash on your face,
From all the sticky, sugary race.
So listen to Mom and eat some fruit,
It's the best thing for you to do!
Swap the candy cane for a juicy orange,
And watch your energy start to soar!
action.label: response
